description: Discovery reduces risk before committing to delivery. These discovery tracks test whether a potential solution is valuable, viable, usable, and feasible.

riskFramework.question: Will customers choose to use it or pay for it?
Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
Can customers figure out how to use it?
Can we build it within given resources and constraints?

items.title: Buyer discovery and trust signals discovery
items.name: Buyer discovery and trust signals discovery
items.summary: Tests whether better category-aware search, fresher alerts, and clearer trust signals increase buyer action in high-volume local categories.
items.opportunity: Clarify whether discovery quality and trust surfaces materially improve buyer conversion without creating excessive moderation or ranking complexity.

linkedInitiatives.description: Improve cross-category local discovery by strengthening search relevance, saved alerts, and buyer trust signals across the buyer web and mobile experiences.

assignments.how: Owns the buyer problem framing, prototype validation, and recommendation.
Provides fraud, identity, and protected-transaction constraints.
Provides relevance, pricing, and supply-signal analysis.

riskFocus.question: Will customers choose to use it or pay for it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Buyers will engage more when search and detail pages make trustworthy local supply easier to judge.
evidenceToCollect: Whether buyers describe noisy discovery and weak trust signals as a top pain.
Whether prototype tests improve save, contact, or return intent.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Improving discovery quality will create enough liquidity and retention to justify ranking and moderation investment.
evidenceToCollect: Expected lift in buyer repeat rate and contact conversion.
Operational cost implications for search and moderation teams.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Buyers can understand stronger trust signals and use richer filters without added confusion.
evidenceToCollect: Where buyers hesitate in filter-heavy flows.
Whether trust indicators help or distract at listing detail level.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can ship a focused buyer discovery slice without a full ranking-platform rewrite.
evidenceToCollect: Dependencies across ranking, messaging, and trust services.
Whether alert precision can be improved with current event quality.

plannedActivities: Interview buyers in electronics, vehicles, and home-goods categories.
Prototype revised search-result and detail-page trust treatments.
Review moderation and ranking dependencies for an MVP release.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to proceed with a broad buyer discovery rollout or limit the work to selected categories.

items.title: Casual seller publish funnel and pricing guidance discovery
items.name: Casual seller publish funnel and pricing guidance discovery
items.summary: Tests whether stronger listing templates, smarter pricing guidance, and clearer trust coaching improve seller completion and sale-through.
items.opportunity: Clarify where the current publish funnel loses casual sellers and whether better price guidance materially improves sale outcomes.

linkedInitiatives.description: Improve publish completion, pricing confidence, and early sale-through by evolving the self-serve listing funnel for casual sellers.

assignments.how: Owns publish-flow research, prototype work, and recommendation.
Provides pricing guidance and demand-signal support.
Provides seller safety and fraud constraints.

riskFocus.question: Will customers choose to use it or pay for it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Casual sellers will publish more often and adopt boosts more readily when pricing and listing quality feel manageable.
evidenceToCollect: Whether sellers cite pricing uncertainty as a top reason for abandonment.
Whether concept tests improve confidence to publish.

riskFocus.question: Will the solution meet the needs of the business?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Publish-flow improvements will increase supply liquidity and paid visibility attach enough to justify the effort.
evidenceToCollect: Impact on publish completion and listing quality.
Impact on boost eligibility and moderation cost.

riskFocus.question: Can customers figure out how to use it?
riskFocus.hypothesis: Casual sellers can complete a category-aware flow faster if the UI hides unnecessary complexity.
evidenceToCollect: Confusion points around price suggestions and condition fields.

riskFocus.question: Can we build it within given resources and constraints?
riskFocus.hypothesis: The teams can reuse existing listing and data infrastructure rather than creating a new seller platform.
evidenceToCollect: Dependencies on media, structured attributes, and analytics.
Feasibility of category-specific templates in existing clients.

plannedActivities: Test publish prototypes with one-off sellers in top casual categories.
Model pricing-guidance quality using existing comparables.
Define the smallest reusable template system that works across web and mobile.
items.decisionToMake: Decide whether to scale a single cross-category seller flow or specialize first for a smaller category set.
